reviews.review_description: Design is thoughtful and so calming. You’re chasing a score but leveling up on your terms. The environment immerses you so well that you feel excited when your town unlocks a tavern, even though there’s no people

Tutorial is easy to understand. Can play without controllers but I recommend using them.  It's a pretty endless city builder/puzzle. You can min max for points or just chill and place buildings. 10/10

reviews.review_title: Super simple and chill city builder
reviews.review_description:
I love this game. I’ve drained my Quest battery two days in a row playing it. And about 5 hours in, I'm done, totally satisfied, ready to move on. It’s a city builder, but incredibly simple. Probably simpler even than Little Cities. There’s no time pressure, no demand, no infrastructure, no traffic, nothing bad ever happens (probably ‘cause there’s also no people). I sit on the floor and play it like I’m eight years old again. It's so clean and uncomplicated that even calling it a city builder might be too much. How about "building placer"?


For anybody who needs a tutorial, here you go: Grip the world and drag it to move around. Turn your palm towards the ceiling to show a building menu. Some buildings like certain buildings, and they hate other buildings. Put them near the buildings they like, and far away from the ones they don’t like, and you’ll get points. Those points will unlock more buildings. Keep going and you’ll unlock other islands to go to, where you’ll also put buildings. That’s it.


To the developer, there are a couple of changes I think would be good.1) The selection ray extends from the wrist, which feels really odd, took me a while to get used to that. Perhaps it should extend from your index finger or from the center of the hand, instead of like I’m Spiderman shooting web. 2) The building menu is often occluded by the hand models, like the hands are transparent but you can't see the menu through them.


And if you love me, hand tracking so I can pick up a building with thumb and forefinger and place it down would be beautiful. 👌

This game is just not very good in the first place because most of the controls don’t work like it takes me ten tries to delete then I have more stuff to delete. This game has good potential it just needs more effort put into it ya know

The game is basic, place a building, it increases score, repeat, game ends when you can't get more buildings, I want a refund, it was an absolute waste of time and money, the only fix is making it more like a building game, add a population happiness, demand, make it us sustaining a settlement or something.
Repetitive, little to no explanation as to how to play the game. I'm surprised at how bad this game is.

It’s fun to build islands but it is also not fun if your not building it for tiny people and make animals to run around and make the game like your a god and you have to protect the people on the island from disasters and evil people.

developer_response.body:
Hello there,

Thank you for this feedback and for the positive review!

reviews.review_description: I bought this to get into strategic games. This is the worst one by far. Don't buy, you work too hard to entertain someone's pockets.
After I  realize that the game was not working I mediately requested I refund. It has been DAYS and I am still trying to get a refund and nothing.  Don’t buy this.

developer_response.body:
Hello, I'm so sorry to hear you are having issues with the game! 

We would love to know what exactly went wrong so that we can make sure the game works smoothly for everyone - if you would like to share more details that would be much appreciated. Thank you in advance and again apologies about your experience so far!

reviews.review_description: I thought this would be a decent version of Little Cities but not very happy with this. Very limited building choices, pretty simplistic, not very intuitive to use. I’ll mess with it some more hoping to find out more but so far I’m glad I didn’t pay full price.

developer_response.body: Hello there, I'm so sorry to hear that the game wasn't quite what you expected! If you have any suggestions on how we can improve your game experience please let us know. We're always open to feedback and suggestions :)

reviews.review_description: I love this game with my all right now. It’s precise feel and calming atmosphere makes it a must have for VR players. I thought about getting little cities and saw that it can stressful so I downloaded this and have yet to regret it. You guys have done a great job and have a masterpiece on your hands! Thank you so much for this, I have enjoyed it a lot so far!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this lovely review and thank you for playing! <3 

A precise feel and calming atmosphere is exactly what we were going for so we're glad to see we succeeded in that aspect!

reviews.review_description: In sandbox mode, you can only have like 100 buildings. Could you make it like 1000? I know it’s a bit much, but please?

developer_response.body: Hello and thanks so much for your review! I will pass on your feedback to the development team :)

reviews.review_description:
✨ l-i-n-k-t-r*e-e/k-e-l-l-0-1-5 ✨

(Remove - and add a point instead of a * to get a surprise! Got a bunch of cozy/sim/adventure/casual games, go take a look if you're interested!)

Nice and cozy game! Loved the PC version, love the VR one
My grandson could work it as and a techie I found it confusing and pointkess without clear easy to understand instructions very disappointed 🙁

developer_response.body:
Hello there! We're very sorry that the game was not clear enough - it is something we are actively reviewing to make sure it is as intuitive and clear as possible. Thank you for sharing your feedback with us and helping us improve the game!

UPDATE - We're pleased to share that the tutorial has now been updated so we would love to hear what you think! We hope this will improve the experience for your grandson.

reviews.review_title: Addictive but relaxing at the same time.
reviews.review_description:
Almost perfect game apart from the tutorial at the beginning isn't clear at all and it was only through trial and error that I worked out the controls but once I got used to them it is top notch playability & engrossing to the max.

Also is definitely a good buy for the price.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this positive review! We couldn't be happier to hear that you enjoyed the game and the feedback about the tutorial has been noted! :)

reviews.review_title: Terrible tutorial. Can't get past it.
reviews.review_description: I'm very proficient in games. But this one is terrible with just symbols for the tutorials on what to do with the controllers at the beginning. I struggled to figure out what the animations meant. The last one where it shows a highlighted button that looks like a sun. I tried everything I knew and can't get that thing to go away. No video reviews out there mention the tutorials and how to get past them. Refunding until this is fixed.

developer_response.body:
Hello,

We're so sorry that the tutorial wasn't clear enough. We will take this feedback on board and see how we can make the tutorial more intuitive. I will also see if I can get some more details about that particular step in the tutorial.

Thank you very much for taking the time to share your feedback and we hope to welcome you back soon! :)

UPDATE - Thanks again for reaching out about the tutorial. We're pleased to say that it has now been updated and we would love to know what you think of it.

reviews.review_description:
I've loved this game ever since I first discovered the (non VR) version several years back. Simple and elegant, easy to learn but difficult to master. Perfect fit for VR and MR. I genuinely hope the developers continue to do more things with it.

I love finally being able to zoom in on my little cities, and I imagine there are always little extra details the developers could add at the smaller scales, like little townsfolk walking around. That said, I don't expect them to add anything like that, as the game does achieve pretty much what they set out to do, and is more about the strategy and simplicity than unnecessary detail.

Coming from Xbox version I had ZERO problems with controls, the tutorial or understanding the game. It’s about a chilled construction of a city, not Civilization. 
But yes, there are bugs. I’m annoyed by losing a selected building just after zooming in or out to find a better spot for it. Night / day transition is a bit abrupt. And yea, there could be a bit more sounds and live to it since we can immerse into it now (sounds of the wind mill or shaman chanting when you get closer to it?). And oh… placing Plateau is pain. 
But these are small annoyances or the overall great zen game.
I got a donut island 🍩.

I always loved sim city and civilization and the like... But they could be stressful. This is the zen version of those games, and you feel accomplished by earning better scores without feeling defeated at any point

developer_response.body:
Hello there!

We're so glad to hear that you are enjoying Islanders! A relaxing yet challenging experience is exactly what we were going for so this is just wonderful to hear. Thank you so much for your review :)
